title: 3 takeaways from the Revolution’s 2-1 win over Minnesota United
author: Hayden Bird
bullets: New England Revolution defeated Minnesota United 2-1 at Gillette Stadium with goals from Carles Gil and Luca Langoni.
Revolution's early penalty by Gil was matched by a Minnesota penalty, but Langoni's second-half goal secured the win.
Matt Turner made crucial saves, helping Revolution hold their lead despite Minnesota's late attacking efforts.

title: SNL recap: Best moments from Will Ferrell hosting ‘Saturday Night Live’ season finale
author: Kevin Slane
bullets: Will Ferrell hosted 'SNL' Season 51 finale on May 16 with musical guest Paul McCartney.
Ferrell's cold open featured him as Jeffrey Epstein's ghost visiting Donald Trump, played by James Austin Johnson.
Paul McCartney performed three songs, including 'Days We Left Behind' and 'Band on the Run,' joined by Chad Smith.

title: Flashback: A look at previous times Tall Ships have sailed into Boston
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: Sail Boston 2026 will occur from July 11 to 16, featuring tall ships from over 25 countries.
The first Sail Boston event in 1976 included Queen Elizabeth II and Prince Philip visiting aboard the royal yacht Britannia.
Sail Boston has historically drawn millions, including controversial events in 1992 and 2000 due to historical slave trade associations.

title: Vibe in Patriots building reportedly better than two weeks ago with Mike Vrabel
author: Colin McCarthy
bullets: Mike Vrabel is participating in the Patriots rookie minicamp, energizing the team after a challenging offseason.
Despite off-field controversies involving Mike Vrabel, reports indicate an improved atmosphere within the Patriots organization.
Kevin Byard supports Vrabel's effectiveness, noting his energetic coaching style continues from his time in Tennessee.

title: Willson Contreras’s go-ahead HR, Payton Tolle’s 8 strong innings lift Red Sox past Braves 3-2
author: Kaley Brown
bullets: Willson Contreras hit a go-ahead 426-foot two-run homer in the eighth inning, leading the Red Sox to a 3-2 win.
Payton Tolle pitched a career-high eight innings for Boston, allowing two runs on four hits and striking out three.
Aroldis Chapman secured the save for the Red Sox, overcoming a bases-loaded situation in the ninth inning.

title: Red Sox place Trevor Story on injured list with sports hernia, contemplating surgery
author: Kaley Brown
bullets: Red Sox placed shortstop Trevor Story on 10-day injured list with a sports hernia.
Injuries impacted Story's performance; he's batting .206 with a league-high error count at shortstop.
Infielder Nick Sogard recalled from Triple-A; Andruw Monasterio replaces Story at shortstop.

title: One driver dead in NH collision between pickup truck and school bus
author: Morgan Rousseau
bullets: A 74-year-old man, David Peloski, died when his pickup truck collided head-on with a school bus in Marlborough, NH.
The incident occurred on Route 12, involving a school bus carrying 13 Monadnock Regional Middle High School students; none were seriously injured.
The crash is under investigation, and authorities request witnesses to contact Trooper Eric Torrens with information.

title: Carl Yastrzemski’s grandson, Braves’ Mike Yastrzemski, walks off Red Sox
author: Kaley Brown
bullets: Mike Yastrzemski's walk-off hit in the 10th secured Braves' 3-2 win against Red Sox.
Red Sox starting pitcher Connelly Early allowed two solo home runs in five innings.
Boston struggled again, leaving seven men on base and batting 1-for-3 with runners in scoring position.

title: Report: Reunion between Patriots, Stefon Diggs viewed as unlikely despite mutual interest
author: Colin McCarthy
bullets: Stefon Diggs is interested in rejoining the Patriots, but a trade for A.J. Brown hinders the possibility.
Albert Breer reports the Patriots may not have cap space for Diggs, assuming they acquire A.J. Brown.
New England is taking on significant salaries, limiting financial flexibility to add Diggs as a third receiver.

title: Boston police seek missing boy, 15
author: Morgan Rousseau
bullets: Lafayette LaGrant-Gomes, 15, was last seen Tuesday near 965 Blue Hill Ave. in Dorchester, Boston police report.
LaGrant-Gomes is described as a Black male, 5'8", with a thin build, brown eyes, and black hair.
Police urge anyone with information to contact District B-3 detectives at 617-343-4712 or submit tips anonymously.
